meaning: Universal Automatic Computer 通用自动计算机
examples: No one, that is, except a Census Bureau computer named Univac.
Remintong Rand, now called Sperr-Rand Corporation, made several second-generation UNIVAC computers.
Univac had been built to tally the census, but its operators programmed it instead to process election results.
The companies making up the first two letters, Burroughs and Univac, combined to form Unisys.
Unlike its research-intensive predecessors, the Univac also was built with business applications in mind.
